description: Aether Vault v1 - Core redaction loop: ingest text files, detect and mask PII with regex + compromise.js, browse masked/raw views, export redacted files. Fully local Tauri desktop app.

userStories.title: Initialize SQLite database and schema on first launch
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Rust backend to create and manage a SQLite database so that vault items and entities can be persisted.
acceptanceCriteria: Add rusqlite dependency to Cargo.toml
On app startup, create SQLite database file in Tauri app data directory if it does not exist
Create items table: id (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY), name (TEXT), file_type (TEXT), raw_content (TEXT), masked_content (TEXT), created_at (TEXT)
Create entities table: id (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY), item_id (INTEGER FOREIGN KEY), entity_type (TEXT), original_value (TEXT), token (TEXT), span_start (INTEGER), span_end (INTEGER)
If database already exists, open it without recreating tables (use CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS)
Database handle is managed as Tauri state and accessible to all commands
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Tauri IPC commands for vault CRUD operations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Rust-side Tauri commands for creating, reading, and deleting vault items so the frontend can interact with the database.
acceptanceCriteria: save_item command: accepts name, file_type, raw_content, masked_content, and a list of entities. Inserts into items and entities tables. Returns the created item id.
list_items command: returns all items (id, name, file_type, entity count, created_at) sorted by created_at DESC. Does NOT return raw_content or masked_content (lightweight list).
get_item command: accepts item id, returns full item with raw_content, masked_content, and all associated entities.
delete_item command: accepts item id, deletes the item and its associated entities in a transaction.
All commands are registered in the Tauri builder
Serde structs defined for request/response types
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Tauri IPC commands for file export and zip archive
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Rust-side commands for saving files to disk and creating zip archives so the frontend can trigger exports.
acceptanceCriteria: Add zip crate dependency to Cargo.toml
export_file command: accepts file name, content, and file extension. Opens native save dialog via Tauri dialog plugin. Writes masked content to chosen path. Default filename is [name]_masked.[ext].
export_zip command: accepts a list of items (name, file_type, masked_content). Opens native save dialog for .zip file. Creates zip archive containing one masked file per item.
Zip file names follow [name]_masked.[ext] format
Zip contains ONLY masked content, no original values
Add tauri-plugin-dialog dependency for native save dialogs
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Regex-based entity detection module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need regex detectors for structured PII so that emails, phone numbers, financial amounts, and API keys are caught reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/detectors/regex.ts module
Detector identifies email addresses (user@domain.tld patterns)
Detector identifies US/international phone numbers (with/without country code, dashes, spaces, parens)
Detector identifies financial amounts ($1,234.56 style with currency symbols $, EUR, GBP)
Detector identifies common API key patterns (AWS AKIA keys, 32+ char hex strings, base64 tokens with key-like prefixes)
Each detection returns: { type: string, value: string, start: number, end: number }
Exported function signature: detectRegex(text: string): Detection[]
Unit tests with node:test cover each entity type with positive matches and negative cases (non-PII text)
pnpm check passes
userStories.notes: All detection runs in the Svelte webview, not in Rust.

userStories.title: NLP-based entity detection with compromise.js
userStories.description: As a developer, I need NLP-based detection for person names and organizations so that unstructured PII is caught beyond what regex can find.
acceptanceCriteria: Add compromise as a dependency in package.json
Create src/lib/detectors/nlp.ts module
Detects person names (first, last, full names) using compromise.people()
Detects organization names using compromise.organizations()
Each detection returns: { type: string, value: string, start: number, end: number }
Exported function signature: detectNlp(text: string): Detection[]
Unit tests with node:test cover person name and org detection with sample paragraphs
pnpm check passes

userStories.title: Detection pipeline: merge regex and NLP results
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a unified detection pipeline that combines regex and NLP results with deduplication so that all entity types are detected in a single pass.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/detectors/index.ts that imports detectRegex and detectNlp
Exported function: detect(text: string): Detection[] runs both detectors
Overlapping spans are deduplicated: if two detections overlap, the longer (more specific) one wins
Results are sorted by start offset ascending
Shared Detection type is defined in src/lib/detectors/types.ts: { type: EntityType, value: string, start: number, end: number }
EntityType is a union: 'EMAIL' | 'PHONE' | 'AMT' | 'API_KEY' | 'PERSON' | 'ORG'
Unit tests verify deduplication and merging with text containing overlapping entities
pnpm check passes

userStories.title: Token masking engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a masking engine that replaces detected entities with [[TYPE_N]] tokens so that sensitive data is redacted consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/masker.ts module
Exported function: mask(text: string, detections: Detection[]): MaskResult
MaskResult contains: { maskedText: string, mappings: { token: string, original: string, type: EntityType, start: number, end: number }[] }
Tokens follow [[TYPE_N]] format (e.g., [[EMAIL_1]], [[PERSON_2]])
Counter increments per type within a single call (first email = [[EMAIL_1]], second = [[EMAIL_2]])
Same entity value appearing multiple times maps to the same token
Masking is idempotent: mask(mask(text)) produces the same output as mask(text) — token patterns [[...]] are not re-detected
Unit tests cover: multi-entity text, duplicate entities, idempotency, and empty input
pnpm check passes

userStories.title: App layout shell and empty state with drop zone
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a clear app layout with a drop zone so I can start adding files immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the Tauri demo page content with the app layout: left sidebar (file list), center (content viewer), right sidebar (entity panel)
When no files exist in the vault, the center area shows a prominent drop zone with dashed border, file icon, and text 'Drop .txt, .md, .csv, or .json files here'
Drop zone accepts .txt, .md, .csv, .json files via HTML drag and drop events
Unsupported file types show an error message on drop
Multiple files can be dropped at once
Drop zone shows a visual hover state (border color change) when files are dragged over it
Basic CSS styling for the three-panel layout (no component library required, plain CSS or scoped styles)
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev tools
userStories.notes: Use Svelte 5 runes ($state, $derived) for reactive state.

userStories.title: Ingestion pipeline: drop files, detect, mask, store
userStories.description: As a user, I want dropped files to be automatically processed and stored so I can see them in my vault.
acceptanceCriteria: When files are dropped on the drop zone, read file contents as text via FileReader API
Run the detect() pipeline on the file text content
Run the mask() engine on the text with detection results
Call the save_item Tauri command via invoke() with: file name, file type, raw content, masked content, and entity list
After saving, refresh the file list in the sidebar
Show a brief loading indicator during processing
Errors during processing display a visible error message
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev tools
userStories.notes: This story wires together the detection, masking, and storage layers built in US-004 through US-007.

userStories.title: Text paste ingestion
userStories.description: As a user, I want to paste raw text into the app so I can redact content that isn't in a file.
acceptanceCriteria: A textarea input is available alongside or within the drop zone area
A submit button processes the pasted text through the same ingestion pipeline (detect, mask, save)
Pasted text is stored as a .txt item with auto-generated name: 'Pasted text - YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM'
After submission, the textarea clears and the new item appears in the file list
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev tools

userStories.title: File browser list view
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all my vault items in a list so I can find and select files to review.
acceptanceCriteria: Left sidebar shows a list of all vault items loaded via list_items Tauri command on app startup
Each item displays: file name, file type badge (.txt, .md, etc.), entity count, and creation date
List is sorted by most recently added (newest first)
Clicking an item selects it (visual highlight) and loads its full data via get_item command
Empty state text shown when no files exist: 'No files in vault'
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev tools

userStories.title: Content viewer with masked/raw toggle
userStories.description: As a user, I want to toggle between masked and raw views of a file so I can verify what was redacted.
acceptanceCriteria: Center content area displays the selected file's content as preformatted text
A toggle button or switch at the top switches between 'Masked' and 'Original' views
Masked view shows the token-redacted text (e.g., [[EMAIL_1]])
Original view shows the raw unredacted text
Default view on selection is Masked
When no file is selected, show a placeholder message
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev tools

userStories.title: Entity highlights and legend in original view
userStories.description: As a user, I want detected entities visually highlighted in the original view so I can see exactly what was redacted.
acceptanceCriteria: In Original view, each detected entity is wrapped in a colored span based on type: blue for EMAIL, green for PERSON, orange for ORG, red for AMT, purple for PHONE, gray for API_KEY
A color legend is visible below the toggle showing entity type to color mapping
Hovering over a highlighted entity shows a tooltip with the token it maps to (e.g., '→ [[EMAIL_1]]')
Non-entity text renders normally between highlighted spans
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev tools

userStories.title: Entity list sidebar panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a summary of all detected entities for the selected file so I can review redactions at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Right sidebar shows entities for the currently selected file
Entities are grouped by type with section headers (EMAIL, PERSON, ORG, AMT, PHONE, API_KEY)
Each entity shows: original value, its token (e.g., [[EMAIL_1]]), and occurrence count in the file
When no file is selected, sidebar shows placeholder text
Groups with zero entities are hidden
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev tools

userStories.title: Export: copy masked text to clipboard
userStories.description: As a user, I want to copy the masked version of a file to my clipboard so I can paste it into an LLM chat.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Copy' button is visible in the content viewer toolbar when a file is selected
Clicking it copies the full masked text to the system clipboard via navigator.clipboard.writeText()
A toast notification appears briefly confirming 'Copied to clipboard'
Toast auto-dismisses after 2 seconds
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev tools

userStories.title: Export: save masked file to disk
userStories.description: As a user, I want to save a masked file to disk so I can use it outside the app.
acceptanceCriteria: An 'Export' button is visible in the content viewer toolbar when a file is selected
Clicking it calls the export_file Tauri command which opens a native save dialog
Default file name is [original_name]_masked.[ext]
File is saved with the same extension as the original
Saved file contains only the masked content
pnpm check passes

userStories.title: Export: batch zip archive
userStories.description: As a user, I want to export all vault items as a zip so I can share a batch of redacted files.
acceptanceCriteria: An 'Export All' button is visible in the file list sidebar header
Clicking it loads full masked content for all items via get_item, then calls export_zip Tauri command
Native save dialog opens for a .zip file
Zip contains one file per vault item named [name]_masked.[ext]
Zip does NOT contain original values or entity mappings
pnpm check passes

userStories.title: Delete vault items
userStories.description: As a user, I want to delete files from my vault so I can manage my stored data.
acceptanceCriteria: A delete button (trash icon or text) is visible per item in the file list
Clicking it shows a confirmation dialog: 'Delete [filename]? This cannot be undone.'
On confirmation, calls delete_item Tauri command with the item id
File list refreshes immediately after deletion
If the deleted item was selected, the content viewer resets to the empty/placeholder state
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev tools
